posts.title: Lesson #376:  Know When to Cut Your Losses
posts.summary: As a founding entrepreneur, it is hard to not to always be “in love” with “your baby”.   You created something from nothing, you nurtured it along the way and you built something really great.   Until that point “your baby” stops growing, your profitability falls with increased competition and the roller coaster starts picking up speed in the wrong direction, with revenues going down, not up as before.   It is very easy to want to “stay the course” and hope for things to get better in the future.   Depending on the root cause of the fall, like a temporary decline in…
posts.author: noreply@blogger.com (George Deeb)

posts.title: Tiny neural net Halloween costumes are the best
posts.summary: I've been experimenting with getting a tiny circa-2015 recurrent neural network to generate Halloween costumes. Running on a single cat hair-covered laptop, char-rnn has no internet training, but learns from scratch to imitate the data I give it. A little while ago I revisited a dataset from 2018,
posts.author: Janelle Shane
tags: Jamm the Hedgehog wants you to keep those pipes flowing and never flush diapers
